trending_topics: AI coding assistants (GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Devin) replacing junior developer tasks

Layoffs in tech attributed partly to AI productivity gains
AI agents capable of autonomous software development (Devin, SWE-agent, OpenAI's coding agents)
Shift in engineering roles toward AI orchestration, prompt engineering, and review rather than writing code
Companies reporting internal AI usage stats (e.g., Google, Microsoft claiming 20-30% of code written by AI)
Backlash and skepticism from senior engineers about AI's real capability vs hype
New job titles emerging: 'AI engineer,' 'prompt engineer,' 'AI orchestrator'
Educational shift: coding bootcamps and CS programs adapting curricula for AI-augmented development
